page: Amazon Web Services r5ad.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring AMD EPYC 7571 processors built on the x86_64 architecture. It provides 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is bundled as 3600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 20 Gbps. The instance runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, including stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression, fall into the bottom 10% tier. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory subsystem performance is a notable strength, with cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth ranking in the top 25%, while uncached large-block read and write bandwidths remain in the middle 50%. Cryptographic capabilities are also strong, with the Passmark CPU encryption test placing in the top 25%, aided by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA_NI instruction sets.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r5ad.24xlarge offers high memory density and bundled high-speed local NVMe storage, reducing the need for external block storage for temporary data. This resource profile is suited for memory-intensive workloads that can leverage parallel processing to overcome lower single-core speeds. Appropriate workloads include large-scale database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, multi-threaded data decompression, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by AMD EPYC 7571 processors on the x86_64 architecture. It delivers 96 vCPUs, 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, managed by the Nitro hypervisor. Network throughput is rated at a baseline of 20 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU execution and text processing are weak, placing in the bottom 10%, whereas multi-core benchmarks and database operations reach average levels. Notably, cached small-block memory bandwidth and CPU encryption performance rank in the top 25%. This configuration provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy deployments by bundling massive RAM and high-speed local storage. It is suited for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching, and parallelized data processing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3600 GB local NVMe SSD storage, offering strong cached memory bandwidth and average multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5ad.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized platform combining ninety-six virtual cores, high-capacity DDR4 memory, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for parallelized data workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 768 GB DDR4 RAM for memory-intensive applications
3600 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD instance storage
Strong cached memory bandwidth and hardware-accelerated encryption
Average multi-core performance contrasting with weak single-core speeds
Suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching, and parallel processing
